title: Construct Handoff Packet
description: Structured artifact emitted at every construct boundary crossing in a composition. Three-tier field policy (required / recommended / optional) per PRD FR-3.1abc. See grimoires/loa/sdd.md §2.4. v1.1 adds source_provenance + kaironic_context + pushback_invitation (additive, backwards-compat). v1.1.1 adds allOf conditionals on provenanceEntry (vault→actor_private + vault!=usable), required fields on kaironicContext + pushbackInvitation, and honest docs about what schema enforces vs what adapter policy enforces. v1.2 adds source_construct_affinity to provenanceEntry + translation_note at envelope root (the construct-DDD anti-corruption layer at handoff — per 'no handoffs without observability' thesis). v1.2.1 closes Flatline+BB findings: privacy_scope on translation_note (privacy stickiness), schema_version coupling allOf (no silent v1.0 producers), tighter slug pattern (no trailing/double hyphens), uniqueItems on affinity arrays, symmetric from/to pairing on translation_note, minLength 60 on translation note (was 30).

output_type.description: Stream type the construct emitted. Required tier.

verdict.description: Construct-specific structured payload. May be empty {} but key MUST be present. Required tier.

invocation_mode.description: How the construct was invoked. Required tier.

cycle_id.description: Identifier for the encompassing cycle (e.g., simstim ID, bridge ID). Required tier.

persona.description: Embodied persona, if any (e.g., ALEXANDER). Recommended tier.

output_refs.description: Outputs the construct produced (file refs, packet refs, content-addressable hashes). Recommended tier.

evidence.description: Citations: file:line refs, transcript excerpts, prior packet IDs. Recommended tier.

source_provenance.description: Source materials the construct consulted, with privacy/use propagation. Set when the construct consumed material from operator-private surfaces (e.g. ~/vault/) or any source whose privacy/authority context should propagate downstream. Schema enforces vault→actor_private and vault!=usable per-entry (see provenanceEntry $def). Adapter-template policy (not schema) determines when emission is required for a given output_type. Recommended tier.

kaironic_context.description: Invocation-timing posture: was this fired kaironically (operator-pulled) vs chronos (scheduled) vs operator-paced. Lets observability detect when scheduled cycles broke operator flow. Lets downstream constructs adjust interrupt-authorization. Optional tier.

pushback_invitation.description: Construct's own self-doubt surfaced for operator review. Schema enforces required: [primary_uncertainty, operator_check] with minLength 20 on each (rejects vague entries like 'I might be wrong'). Adapter-template policy determines when output_type='Verdict' on high-blast-radius outputs must populate this field. Composes with operator OS MAY-LATITUDE-5 'pushback affordance' rule. Recommended tier.

translation_note.description: Construct-DDD anti-corruption layer at envelope-level handoff (v1.2). When an envelope crosses a construct boundary (calling construct's room hands off to a different construct's room), this field carries the translation surface — how key terms from the emitting construct's bounded context map into the receiving construct's. Same word means different things across constructs (e.g., 'weight' in artisan vs noether); translation_note makes the translation visible and auditable. Composes with [[no-handoffs-without-observability]] thesis. Recommended tier when from_construct != to_construct in a composition.

agent_id.description: Claude Code subagent ID when interactive. Null when headless. Optional tier.

transcript_path.description: Local-machine path to subagent transcript. See SDD §2.4.1a access policy. Optional tier.

transcript_excerpt.description: Sanitized highlights for cross-tool sharing. Preferred over transcript_path when packets cross machine boundaries. Optional tier.

input_refs.description: Inputs the construct consumed. Optional tier.

open_questions.description: AskUserQuestion-class items the construct deferred. Optional tier.

gates_passed.description: Gates the construct met. Optional tier.

gates_failed.description: Gates the construct failed (with reason). Optional tier.

composition_run_id.description: Composition run identifier when chained. Optional tier.

stage_index.description: Zero-based stage index in a composition. Optional tier.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with explicit Z. Validator MAY auto-populate if missing. Optional tier.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this packet. Always 1.x.y for this cycle. v1.0 = original; v1.1 = adds source_provenance + kaironic_context + pushback_invitation; v1.1.1 = adds allOf privacy invariants + required fields on new defs; v1.2 = adds source_construct_affinity + translation_note (construct-DDD anti-corruption layer); v1.2.1 = closes Flatline+BB findings (privacy_scope on translation_note, schema_version allOf coupling, tighter slug pattern, uniqueItems, symmetric from/to pairing, minLength 60). Optional tier (defaults to 1.0). v1.2.1 enforces via allOf: if any v1.2+ field is present, schema_version MUST be >= 1.2.

allOf.$comment: v1.2.1: if any v1.2 field is present (translation_note OR source_construct_affinity on any provenanceEntry), schema_version MUST match ^1\.([2-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) so v1.0/v1.1-pinned consumers can refuse cleanly. Closes BB-medium-no-version-gate-on-translation-note + Flatline-IMP-010.

type.description: Reference category (e.g., file, packet, transcript, url, sha256).

hash.description: SHA-256 content hash for content-addressable refs. Null when not applicable.

source_type.description: Origin class. 'vault' = raw operator-private second-brain content (~/vault/) — schema enforces privacy_scope=actor_private AND use_label!=usable (raw vault content can never be authority). 'activated_doctrine' = vault citation post-operator-activation receipt — MAY carry use_label=usable (operator promoted it for this room/cycle). 'grimoire' = project-scoped org state. 'session' = prior session episodic record. 'agent_inference' = no external source, agent-derived. 'external' = third-party (papers, repos, docs).

ref.description: Source reference (path, URL, or named identifier). For vault sources, vault-relative path (e.g. 'wiki/concepts/freeside-as-layered-station.md'). For activated_doctrine, receipt_id of the activation event.

privacy_scope.description: Propagated downstream. Composes with Eileen's recall-wedge SignatureEnvelope privacy semantics (loa-straylight). actor_private MUST NOT flow to public-frame surfaces. Schema-enforced for vault source_type via allOf.

use_label.description: Authority level the source carries in the current room. Vault sources (raw) MUST NOT be 'usable' (schema-enforced via allOf); promote via source_type=activated_doctrine. Composes with Operator OS Straylight Memory Discipline.

operator_validated.description: Whether the operator has personally read and validated this source. Distinct from operator_signed (cryptographic). Maps to vault frontmatter read_state >= 'read'.

source_construct_affinity.description: Construct-DDD affinity (v1.2): which construct(s)' bounded context the source belongs to. Slugs match installed construct.yaml entries (artisan, observer, k-hole, the-arcade, noether, etc.) OR project anchors (purupuru, mibera-honeyroad, freeside, etc.) OR the literal sentinel 'cross-domain' for spanning doctrine. Applies to ALL source_type values — for vault sources reflects the vault page's construct_affinity frontmatter; for non-vault sources reflects the interpreting construct domain. Mirrors vault frontmatter `construct_affinity`. Slug existence validation against active construct registry is NOT enforced at schema level (caller responsibility; future build-time check). v1.2.1: uniqueItems prevents duplicate slugs; tighter pattern rejects trailing/double hyphens.

decay_class.description: OPTIONAL ARCHIVIST EXTENSION. Ebbinghaus decay class for confidence aging per the archivist construct's lifecycle model. Universal substrate constructs MUST NOT require this field; consumers not running archivist may omit. Marked as construct-specific extension per BB-F5 / author self-flag — generic substrate alternative: omit and let archivist emit its own side-channel envelope when needed.

hash.description: SHA-256 of the source content at time of consultation. Lets downstream detect drift.

allOf.$comment: Closes BB-F1 / Flatline-SKP-001 vault-leak vector: raw vault sources MUST be actor_private.
Closes Flatline-SKP-001-other authority contradiction: raw vault is never usable; promotion requires source_type=activated_doctrine.

trigger.description: Why this construct fired. 'kaironic' = operator in flow. 'chronos' = scheduled (cron, autonomous, post-merge). 'operator_invoked' = explicit operator command. 'upstream_handoff' = composed downstream of another room.

flow_state.description: Operator's working-state classification at invocation. Mirrors operator OS modes (DIG/ARCH/SHIP/etc.) but normalized for envelope use.

attention_cost.description: Cost of the downstream operator-facing summary if this envelope surfaces. Low = single fact. Medium = synthesis. High = multi-page crystallization.

interrupt_authorized.description: ADVISORY ONLY. Construct's suggestion about whether mid-flight surfacing is appropriate. The orchestrator owns the final interruption decision per Operator OS Kaironic Time Protocol — this field is a hint, not authorization. Defaults to false. Per BB-F11 review: orchestrator MUST NOT delegate authorization to emitters.

from_construct.description: Slug of the emitting construct's bounded context. Optional when the envelope is operator-direct.

to_construct.description: Slug of the receiving construct's bounded context. Optional when the envelope is for any consumer.

note.description: Free-form translation note: how key terms from the emitting construct's ubiquitous language map to the receiving construct's. One paragraph. v1.2.1: minLength bumped from 30 → 60 to force substantive translations (closes BB-low-minlength-30). Required field when translation_note is present.

privacy_scope.description: v1.2.1: privacy scope of the translation note itself. MUST inherit the most-restrictive privacy_scope across all source_provenance entries the note translates from (caller responsibility — schema-level cross-field comparison not natively expressible; future build-time check). When omitted, downstream consumers MUST treat the translation as actor_private (fail-closed default). Closes Flatline-SKP-001-CRITICAL: privacy leak through translation_note free-form `note` field could inline actor_private vault content.

key_translations.description: Optional structured term-by-term translation table. Each row: a term, what it means in from_construct, what it means in to_construct. v1.2.1: uniqueItems prevents duplicate term rows. Surfaces the explicit anti-corruption mapping.

allOf.$comment: v1.2.1: from_construct and to_construct must be paired symmetrically — both absent, both null, or both present as strings. Closes BB-medium-translation-note-pair-not-enforced + Flatline-SKP-002 partial.

primary_uncertainty.description: What the construct is least confident about. One sentence. NOT 'I'm pretty sure but...' — name the actual weak link in the verdict. minLength 20 rejects vague entries like 'I might be wrong' (per BB-F3 review).

operator_check.description: Single specific question the operator should verify before accepting. Bad: 'is this OK?'. Good: 'is the assumption that <X> holds in your environment correct?'. minLength 20 rejects trivial yes/no prompts.

alternative_paths.description: Up to 4 alternative paths the construct considered and rejected. Surfaces counterfactuals for operator review.

confidence.description: Self-reported confidence in the verdict, 0..1. Calibratable over time.
